title: Eupatorium tepicanum
intro: Eupatorium tepicanum, also known as Mexican Joe Pye Weed, is a perennial plant native to Mexico, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Eupatorium tepicanum is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its health benefits, which are also used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Eupatorium tepicanum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central America, specifically in Costa Rica. The classification of this plant is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the order Asterales. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification provides a clear understanding of the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

preparations: Eupatorium tepicanum is a plant species, but specific information about its herbal preparations could not be found.
